description: 48M with well-controlled T2DM on metformin, A1C 6.5%, no CVD, no CKD, eGFR 95, LDL 110, ASCVD 5.2%. The 'boring' patient — tests correct non-activation of most guidelines. ADA R5 (statin for diabetes 40-75) fires for moderate-intensity statin. ACC/AHA R3 (diabetes statin) also fires — convergence. ACC/AHA R4 does not fire (requires no diabetes). USPSTF does not fire (ASCVD 5.2% < 7.5% for Grade C, < 10% for Grade B). KDIGO does not fire (eGFR 95, no CKD). ADA R2/R3 do not fire (no ASCVD/HF/CKD). ADA R4 does not fire (A1C < 7%). ADA R1 does not fire (already on metformin).

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy for diabetes
actions.rationale: ADA R5 (ADA-A): moderate-intensity statin for all diabetic adults 40-75 regardless of ASCVD risk score. ACC/AHA R3 (COR I, LOE A) converges: moderate-intensity statin for diabetes 40-75. ASCVD 5.2% is below the 7.5% threshold for high-intensity consideration, so moderate-intensity is appropriate. LDL 110 is modestly above 100 but without additional risk enhancers (no HTN, no smoking, ASCVD 5.2%), moderate-intensity is appropriate.

actions.label: Continue metformin and diabetes monitoring
actions.rationale: A1C 6.5% — at or below 7% target. Well-controlled on metformin monotherapy. eGFR 95 — no dose adjustment needed. Continue current regimen with routine A1C monitoring.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend statin based on USPSTF guidance
contraindications.rationale: ASCVD 5.2% is below both USPSTF Grade B (>=10%) and Grade C (7.5-<10%) thresholds. USPSTF statin recommendations do not fire for this patient.

contraindications.label: Do not prescribe high-intensity statin
contraindications.rationale: No diabetes-specific risk enhancers (LDL 110, no HTN, no smoking, ASCVD 5.2%). ACC/AHA R3 COR IIa high-intensity escalation requires ASCVD >=7.5% or multiple risk factors. Moderate-intensity is the guideline-concordant choice.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend SGLT2i
contraindications.rationale: No ASCVD, no HF, no CKD (eGFR 95). ADA R2 criteria not met. KDIGO not applicable. A1C at target — no glycemic intensification needed (R4 ineligible).

contraindications.label: Do not recommend GLP-1 RA
contraindications.rationale: No established ASCVD. Age 48 — below 55 threshold for ADA R3 high-risk path. A1C at target — no glycemic intensification needed. No indication for GLP-1 RA.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend adding a second glucose-lowering agent
contraindications.rationale: ADA R4 requires A1C >=7% on metformin. A1C 6.5% is below target. Patient is at goal on monotherapy.
